fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 70 uses strict greater-than (`> limit`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`>= limit`), so when the number of recent pings exactly equals the limit the function incorrectly allows the request instead of denying it.

constraints.intent: When recent ping count equals the limit the condition should be True (i.e., the branch should be taken to deny); the strict > causes it to fall through incorrectly.
After the else branch (condition false), recent count must be strictly less than limit for the allow+remaining calculation to be correct.
Sanity check that the comparison threshold used later (>= vs >) correctly partitions the count relative to limit; specifically that equal-to-limit is handled by the deny branch.

spec.expr: not (__cbfl_result[0] is True and len(recent__1) >= limit)

constraints.intent: The function must never return allowed=True when the recent ping count has already reached or exceeded the limit.
